summary: Add the set-scope `unique` roster predicate — a declared field must be unique across a role's matched set.

per.section: The set scope (the roster) — predicates over a matched set

edit.description: Add an optional `unique: Vec<String>` (declared field names) to `Role`; parse a `unique = ["field", …]` array in `parse_role`, folding any malformation (not an array, a non-string element) into a new `ComposeError::RoleBadUnique` variant; absent ⇒ empty vec. Add the `unique: Vec::new()` field to the `Role { … }` struct literals in this module's unit tests, and add a parse unit test.

Extend `check` (already wired in `main.rs`): after the count/required arm, for each declared `unique` field group the role's matched fillers by that field's extracted scalar value (`Features::field` + `as_scalar`) and emit one error `Diagnostic` per duplicated value under a new `ROLE_UNIQUE_RULE` (`role.unique`), naming the role, field, shared value, and colliding fillers. Add unit tests covering a collision firing, distinct values staying silent, and a missing field being no collision.

tests.asserts: A role declaring `unique = ["model"]` fires an error-severity `role.unique` finding when two matched fillers share a `model` value, names the colliding fillers, and is silent when every matched filler's value differs.
`unique = ["model"]` parses into `Role.unique`; an absent `unique` defaults to an empty vec; a non-array or non-string-element `unique` is a `RoleBadUnique` load error.

acceptance: A `temper.toml` role declaring `unique = ["model"]` reports an error when two matched fillers share a `model` value and is silent when all differ; a malformed `unique` is a load error.
notes: Set-scope `unique` generalizes the kind-wide `unique-name` engine predicate (`Predicate::UniqueName`, name-only, over a whole kind) to an arbitrary field over a *role's* matched subset — a roster-tier check, not an engine one. Folds into the existing `roster::check` call so it needs no `main.rs` edit, keeping it disjoint from the parked SPEC-KIND-GATE (main.rs). Decidable: exact equality grouping over extracted scalars, a true positive every time (`00-intent.md` law 3).

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor, shipped — `extract::spec_features` on disk) plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
